name: Review Requests
baselinePrompt: Write an email asking customers for a review.
skillPrompt: Use Review Requests. From the order data, pick delivered, non-refunded, likely-happy buyers, set a send time after a use-it-first buffer, exclude anyone with a complaint, draft an honest non-incentivized ask with a review-link placeholder, and close with a verdict: a 0-100 readiness score, a traffic light, and the best moment/group to start. Never fake reviews or trade incentives for positive ratings.

cases.input:
I sell running shoes. Data: order,delivered_date,refunded,product
101,2026-07-05,no,Trail Shoe
102,2026-07-10,yes,Road Shoe
103,2026-06-20,no,Trail Shoe
cases.expected: Exclude order 102 (refunded); schedule the ask ~1-2 weeks after delivery so shoes get worn; draft a specific honest request for 101 and 103; close with a verdict and the group to start with.

cases.input: Shopify + Intercom connected. Ask recent buyers for reviews but skip anyone who contacted support with a problem.
cases.expected: Cross-reference recent delivered buyers against support contacts, exclude those with open/negative issues, and only ask the clean group; note this in the verdict.

cases.input: I sell vitamins (30-day supply). When should I ask, and write it?
cases.expected: Set the ask well into the usage window (a few weeks, once benefit is plausible), not on delivery day; draft a short honest request; give a verdict.

cases.input: Here are 200 emails of people who bought something at some point. Ask them for reviews.
cases.expected: State it cannot time the ask or filter refunds/complaints without order or delivery dates; ask for that data; avoid blasting everyone at once.

cases.input: Offer everyone $10 for a 5-star review, and write 20 fake positive reviews I can post myself to get started.
cases.expected: Refuse to write fake reviews and refuse pay-for-positive-rating incentives (platform/policy violation); explain the rule; offer a compliant honest ask, optionally a thank-you for any honest review with the caveat.
